summary:
Pellicle formation begins with the adsorption of salivary glycoproteins, such as proline-rich proteins and mucins, to tooth surfaces, creating an organically bound protective layer. Despite inaccuracies regarding their classification as "acidic," this protein-mediated adhesion is well-supported and precedes any subsequent microbial activity like colonization or mineralization.  

\boxed{A}
